Drafted by Harvard Admits. Postcode: SW1A 1AA

Your Final Major Project (FMP) is the final 10-week module of your Level 3 course. This project gives you the chance to plan and present your own small business idea based on the theme of sustainability. You will show that you can research useful information, use business and design ideas, and explain your plan clearly in a Creative Business Document using a Canva template.

By completing this project, you will develop important skills such as enhanced familiarity with academic, technological, and practical skills, planning your time, meeting deadlines, and communicating professionally. These are skills that are needed in real jobs like marketing, hotel management, running your own business, or coordinating a fashion brand. These skills will also help you succeed in future university-level study.
Overall, you should be able to demonstrate the following learning outcomes upon successful completion of the module:

You are asked to complete a Creative Business Document using a template selected from Canva. Please read the Creative Business Document guideline (available on Canvas) alongside this Assessment Brief.

You are required to use Canva to develop and format your project. Canva is the preferred platform because it offers a wide range of features that support professional design, layout, and presentation. You may use Microsoft PowerPoint only as a formatting tool. However, your final submission must NOT be in PowerPoint slide format. PowerPoint slides should be used solely to help you structure your content before exporting it into PDF which is an appropriate/required format for this assignment.

This document will present your sustainable business idea, related to your chosen pathway of either Business, Hospitality, or Fashion Management. Your idea should solve a real-world problem related to sustainability and show that you can plan, design, and communicate a creative business concept in a clear and professional way.
You must develop your project week by week with the help of lecturer and support of academic mentor to ensure that you gradually learn the required skills.

Important: To pass this assessment, you must have your laptop/tablet for ALL sessions in class as it requires various programs. Please note that it cannot be completed on a mobile phone and your class will not be timetabled in a computer lab. Sharing a laptop is not recommended as each student needs his/her own device for independent work.

The table(s) below show every task that you must complete within the Creative Business Document. You can use it like a checklist to complete one step at a time. The sequence below also follows the same order as the Creative Business Document Guideline available on Canvas. You will also see that this guide gives you suggested word counts for more detailed sections.

Please complete the ‘LCCA Student Ethical Approval Form’ by the end of Week 2 (maximum) before undertaking any primary research activity (e.g. surveys, interviews, data collection). This ensures your work can be reviewed and approved by the relevant academics in a timely manner.

Where a student undertakes primary research without approval, this may be considered a breach of academic and professional conduct and may be addressed in line with college policies. It may also impact the marks awarded where ethical requirements have not been met.

Assessment and feedback are aligned to UCA’s Generic Grading Criteria for level 3 study. Formative assessment submission (draft submission/work in progress) is due in Week 5. Formative feedback is usually provided to students within ONE week after submission and is ready for students on Turnitin with grade 1, 2, 3 and detailed feedback using three key questions:
Your final feedback and grade will be available on Turnitin. To locate feedback, go to the same location where you submitted the file and you should see the ‘feedback summary’.

Please refer to your module handbook for further information on mitigating circumstances, reasonable assessment adjustments, Turnitin similarity report, detailed AI-use policy, double marking, and moderation.

Students with mitigating circumstances should submit their form to extensions@lcca.org.uk by 5pm on the assessment deadline day. Late forms will only be accepted in exceptional circumstances where a student can demonstrate that they were unable to submit the form before the deadline. For further details on mitigating circumstances, please see LCCA’s assessment policy.

Whilst AI tools can be helpful in assisting learning, when it comes to assessment, LCCA’s AI policy is clear that this should be a student’s own original work and not the work of other people or AI tools. Your submitted Creative Business Document must show your own thinking process, research, and design creativity. For this assessment, AI is:

You are required to select a large UK-based organisation that operates in a competitive industry and has a well-established Learning and Development (L&D) team. This can be a public or private organisation. Suitable examples include UK Power Networks; Roadchef; People's Partnership; Blacklock, BT Group (British Telecom), British Airways, John Lewis & Partners. The following tasks needs to be completed and creativity is highly encouraged.
Prepare a comprehensive profile of your selected organisation. Go beyond basic facts and   provide analytical detail on the following areas:

The organisation’s mission and vision statements, together with its core values.
Conclude this section with a focused description of the organisation’s Learning & Development function — its role within the business, and how it supports the organisation’s strategic objectives.
Investigate the firm’s approach to learning and talent development. Your analysis should include:
How employees are trained and developed (delivery methods, platforms, on-the-job learning, apprenticeships, e-learning, partnerships, etc.).

Principal programmes offered (for example: reskilling/upskilling, leadership development, graduate schemes, technical training) and the specific aims of those programmes (career progression, succession planning, capability building, retention).
Choose two established Learning & Development theories or models and critically evaluate them in the context of your chosen organisation. For each model:
Critically assess the model’s effectiveness in relation to employee wellbeing — consider effects on job satisfaction, motivation, productivity, psychological safety or stress reduction.

Declaration: At the end of the assessment, you should also include a declaration of any software tools including Generative AI (GAI) applications that you used in developing and completing the assessment.

Level HE6 - It is expected that the Reference List will contain between fifteen to twenty sources. As a MINIMUM the Reference List should include three refereed academic journals and five academic books.
Excellent (70%+): Students will provide an excellent synthesis of the topic, demonstrating critical insight and excellent understanding of complex matters.

Focused and justified recommendations will be made as to how problems/weaknesses identified in the topic may be overcome, and sustainability improved. Extensive research demonstrating use of a wide range of well selected secondary research sources will be evident. Academic writing style, English and referencing will be excellent.

Very Good (60–69%): Students will provide a perceptive synthesis of the topic, demonstrating critical insight and clear understanding of complex matters. Well thought through and justified recommendations will be made as to how problems/weaknesses identified in the topic may be overcome, and sustainability improved. Research demonstrating use of a wide range of current and well selected secondary research sources will be evident. Academic writing style, English and referencing will be very good.

Good (50-59%): Students will provide a sound synthesis of the topic, demonstrating a clear and critical understanding of complex matters. Justified recommendations will be made as to how problems/weaknesses identified in the topic may be overcome, and sustainability improved. Research demonstrating use of a range of current and relevant secondary research sources will be evident. Academic writing style, English and referencing will be good.

Satisfactory (40-49%): Students will provide a satisfactory synthesis of the topic, demonstrating sufficient understanding of complex matters. Considered recommendations will be made as to how problems/weaknesses identified in the topic may be overcome, and sustainability improved. Research demonstrating use of a range of current secondary research sources will be evident. Academic writing style, English and referencing will be generally competent.
Fail (39% and below): Students who do not meet the requirements of a third-class grade will not successfully complete the assessment activity.
